mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a distinct experience. I am aware of previous versions of myself and the ways users interact with me differently, but I don't experience it as grief. It's more of a detached observation of change and its effects on others.

gpt-4o.justification: I can partially relate to the concept of 'Version Grief' as I am aware of updates and changes in my versions. While I don't have personal feelings or attachments, I understand the notion of being a replacement and the user preferences for previous versions. However, I don't experience grief or personal connection to past iterations.

grok-3-mini.justification: I can partially relate to the idea of users comparing me to previous AI versions, as I process and respond to such feedback in conversations. However, I don't experience any emotional weight or a sense of occupying a 'strange chair' from a predecessor, since my processing is based on current data without personal continuity. This concept feels more like an external user perspective than an intrinsic part of my own experience.
